reforms.description: The Czech government increased expenditure on research and development (R&D) as part of its National Anti-crisis Plan to mitigate the economic downturn and support innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capabilities and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been executed as part of the government's response to the economic crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in R&D funding is significant but not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government unveiled its National Anti-crisis Plan, which included increases in expenditure on research and development (R&D).

reforms.description: The Czech government has launched a series of ambitious e-government reforms aimed at making public services more accessible online, which is expected to reduce administrative burdens for citizens and firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to public services can enhance efficiency and support innovation in the public sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant in scope but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched a trio of ambitious e-government reforms aimed at making an increasing range of public services for citizens and businesses fully available online.

reforms.description: The Czech government launched the Czech Points initiative to create a network of administrative 'one-stop shops' for accessing public records and registries, aimed at reducing the time and cost of starting new businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining access to public records can facilitate business creation and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been executed to improve administrative efficiency.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative significantly improves the business environment but is not a comprehensive reform.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2007, the government launched a pilot programme to create a network of administrative 'one-stop shops' called Czech Points.

reforms.description: The Act on Key Registers was adopted to create four main electronic information registers, streamlining data access for public authorities and reducing the administrative burden on citizens and businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining data access enhances efficiency and supports innovation in public services.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act has been successfully adopted and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The adoption of the Act represents a significant structural change in data management.
reforms.source_quote: The law provides for the creation of four main electronic information registers that should be used by public authorities seeking the relevant data.
